chunks.text: Andrew R. Neureuther is Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley's Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences, where he joined in 1966 as the Conexant Systems Distinguished Professor. He earned B.S., M.S., and Ph.D. degrees in Electrical Engineering from the University of Illinois, Urbana, in 1963, 1964, and 1966, respectively, as part of the Antenna Laboratory.
chunks.question: What is Andrew Neureuther's academic position and where did he earn his degrees?
chunks.answer: Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley; degrees from University of Illinois.

chunks.text: Dr. Neureuther pioneered modeling and simulation of integrated circuit processing for physical process effects and innovation in emerging technologies. His work includes models for chemically amplified imaging materials (STORM), simulation of optical, electron, ion beam and x-ray lithography (SAMPLE), assessment of residual effects of defects and lens aberrations (SPLAT), electromagnetic scattering (TEMPEST), time-evolution of topography (SAMPLE3D), environments for integrating simulators with process flow (SIMPL), and remote web-based simulation (LAVA).
chunks.question: What areas did Andrew Neureuther pioneer in integrated circuit processing?
chunks.answer: Modeling and simulation for various physical process effects.

chunks.text: Dr. Neureuther is a Fellow of the IEEE, published 250 papers, advised 35 M.S. and 30 Ph.D. students, and was elected to the National Academy of Engineering in 1995. He received the IEEE 2003 Cledo Brunetti field award for contributions to miniaturization in electronics, the Berkeley Citation in 2007, and the Frits Zernike Award for Microlithography in 2011. Dr. Neureuther passed away in September 2025.
chunks.question: What are some key honors and when did Andrew Neureuther pass away?
chunks.answer: IEEE Fellow, NAE member; passed away September 2025.

chunks.text: His research areas include Integrated Circuits (INC) and Solid-State Devices. He was associated with the research center Integrated Modeling Process and Computation for Technology (IMPACT). Selected publications span from 1969 to 2009, covering topics like predictive compact modeling for strain effects in nanoscale transistors, characterizing aberrations in imaging lenses, and modeling for nanometrics.
chunks.question: What were Andrew Neureuther's research areas and associated center?
chunks.answer: Integrated Circuits, Solid-State Devices; IMPACT research center.

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards include the SRC Aristotle Award (2012), SRC Inventor Recognition Award (2010), Berkeley Citation (2007), SIA University Research Award (2007), IEEE Cledo Brunetti Award (2003), National Academy of Engineering Member (1995), and IEEE Fellow (1989).
chunks.question: List some awards Andrew Neureuther received from 2003 to 2012.
chunks.answer: IEEE Cledo Brunetti Award, Berkeley Citation, SRC awards.
